trivia_bullets.text: Revitalize debuted in Core Set 2019 (July 2018), reprinted in Core Set 2021 (July 2020), Kaldheim (February 2021), and twice in Strixhaven Mystical Archive (April 2021) — including a Japanese alternate-art version in collector boosters (card no. 072). Reprinted again in Foundations Jumpstart (November 2024). One of the more frequently reprinted white utility commons in recent Magic history.

Flavor text: '"A potion is no substitute for a skilled surgeon, but it will hold your bits in." — Torricks, battlefield medic'. Torricks is a named but otherwise unattested figure in Wizards lore — the name appears only in this flavor text across all Magic printings. The voice is pragmatic about magic: the potion as damage-control, not salvation.

Matt Stewart has created over 200 Magic: The Gathering illustrations. His 'Master Trinketeer' (Kaladesh) received a Chesley Award nomination and was cited by Hipsters of the Coast as among the best MTG artworks since 2012. 'Waste Not' (You Make the Card community design) was noted as a personal favorite.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: The Best Magic The Gathering Artworks Since 2012, 2022-09
Hipsters of the Coast: A Look Inside Whimsy Wonder & War, 2019-11

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 4156 — substantial presence in lifegain-payoff Commander builds. The baseline ({1}{W}: gain 3 life, draw a card) has proven durable enough to earn reprints across four years and a Mystical Archive treatment that elevated the art to premium collector status.

related_cards.name: Healing Salve
related_cards.note: Ancestral comparison: same 3-life gain, no draw attached — Revitalize strictly outpaces it

related_cards.name: Chaplain's Blessing
related_cards.note: Same 3-life gain, same mana cost, no draw — Revitalize is the direct design upgrade

related_cards.name: Revitalize (Kaldheim)
related_cards.note: Same card, new art by Mathias Kollros (no. 23); one of three distinct art versions across the print run
